---
title: "Tiffany Wu"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: ProfilePic_UMIch.JPG
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/tiffany-wu-854b775a/
    - icon: google
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=_ZaBJzYAAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/tiffany-wu
css: styles.css
toc: false
---

<div class="about-bio">

Welcome! 👋

I am currently a Postdoctoral Fellow at [Carnegie Mellon University's Human-Computer Interaction Institute](https://hcii.cmu.edu/), where I work with [Dr. Ken Koedinger](https://hcii.cmu.edu/people/ken-koedinger) and [Dr. Danielle Thomas](https://hcii.cmu.edu/people/danielle-thomas) on the [PLUS Project](https://www.tutors.plus/), a hybrid human-AI tutoring program that aims to reduce opportunity gaps in math education by combining the complementary strengths of human tutors and AI.

Broadly, my research weaves AI (often in the form of machine learning), causal inference, and psychometric methods to design, evaluate, and scale PK-12 educational tools and interventions. My work to date has spanned three substantive areas: chronic absenteeism, early childhood education policy, and math education. I am deeply committed to leveraging advances in data science and AI to advance educational opportunity and equity. Through research-practice partnerships, I aim to produce research that is rigorous, accessible, and grounded in the lived experiences of students, families, and practitioners across diverse educational contexts.

My research has been deeply influenced by my work as a public high school teacher in Chicago and then as a research analyst for an education technology non-profit prior to starting my doctoral training.

I received my PhD in [Education & Psychology](https://soe.umich.edu/academics-admissions/cpep) along with a dual master's degree in [Statistics](https://lsa.umich.edu/stats/masters_students/mastersprograms/dualmasters.html) from the University of Michigan, where I was very fortunate to be advised by [Dr. Christina Weiland](https://sites.google.com/a/umich.edu/christina-weiland/). During this time, I was an [IES Causal Inference Predoctoral Fellow](https://edpolicy.umich.edu/training/predoctoral-fellowship) and an [APPAM Equity and Diversity Fellow](https://www.appam.org/about-appam/awards/2022-equity-inclusion-fellowship-student-recipients/). I also hold a B.S. in Secondary Education & History from [Northwestern University](https://sesp.northwestern.edu/) and an M.A. in Psychology from the [University of Chicago](https://mapss.uchicago.edu/).

Outside of work, I like to paint, play the piano, and play pickleball.

## News

-   **June 2026:** Started as a Postdoctoral Fellow at [Carnegie Mellon University's Human-Computer Interaction Institute](https://hcii.cmu.edu/).
-   **June 2026:** Together with [Dr. Christina Weiland](https://sites.google.com/a/umich.edu/christina-weiland/) and Jonas Xie, developed the [West Virginia School Consolidations Dashboard](https://wvschoolconsolidations.shinyapps.io/westvirginiaschoolclosures/). Learn more about it [here](https://westvirginiawatch.com/2026/06/04/wv-native-creates-online-state-school-closure-tracker-70-of-closures-impacted-elementary-schools/).
-   **May 2026:** Received my PhD in [Education & Psychology](https://soe.umich.edu/academics-admissions/cpep) and dual master's in [Statistics](https://lsa.umich.edu/stats/masters_students/mastersprograms/dualmasters.html) from the University of Michigan!
-   **April 2026:** Presented at the [EveryDay Labs](https://www.everydaylabs.com/) webinar on [Is 10% too late? New research on chronic absenteeism measurement](https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_0_osS5MPQxKthCTXxlZgcQ#/registration)— thanks for having me!
-   **April 2026:** Defended my PhD dissertation, entitled *Showing Up is Half the Battle: Leveraging Machine Learning, Measurement, and Causal Inference to Improve PK-12 Student Attendance.* Read the first paper (published in *EEPA*) [here](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/pdf/10.3102/01623737261419046). More to come soon!
-   **March 2026:** Presented at a District Administration & HopSkipDrive webinar on [Rethinking the 10% absence benchmark: New attendance research and what districts can do ](https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/5259302/8233E843590106B203E9A86DCCDBAA58?partnerref=DAPreBannerAD)— thanks for having me!


<details>
<summary>Older updates</summary>

- **2025:** Awarded the William C. Morse Fellowship, University of Michigan ($995).
- **2024:** Received the [Sarri Family Fellowship for Research on Educational Attainment of Children in Low-Income Families, University of Michigan Institute for Social Research ](https://cps.isr.umich.edu/fellowships/sarri-family-fellowship/) ($6,000).
- **2024:** First first author paper officially published in *Assessment*. [Read it here](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/pdf/10.1177/10731911241229566)!
- **2024:** Received the Dr. Stuart A. Karabenick Fellowship, University of Michigan ($1,000).
- **2023–2025:** Received the Rackham Conference Travel Grant, University of Michigan ($900 annually).
- **2022:** Awarded (co-PI) the *Collaborative Partnership with Local Michigan Schools to Promote Interest in STEM* DEI Faculty Grant from the University of Michigan with [Dr. Salar Fattahi](https://fattahi.engin.umich.edu/) ($10,000). [Read about our workshop here](https://ioe.engin.umich.edu/2023/04/18/the-university-of-michigan-welcomes-local-high-school-students-to-explore-real-life-applications-of-machine-learning-in-engineering/)!
- **2022:** Selected as an IES Causal Inference in Education Policy Research Predoctoral Fellow (four years of doctoral support; approximately $146,000).
- **2022:** Selected as an [APPAM Equity and Inclusion Student Fellow](https://www.appam.org/about-appam/awards/2022-equity-inclusion-fellowship-student-recipients/) ($600).
- **2022:** Received the ISR–Rackham Summer Training Award, University of Michigan ($3,800).
- **2021:** Awarded the Rackham Regents Fellowship, University of Michigan (one year of doctoral support; $32,691).


</details>

</div>
